title: Sentinel Shield accepted risks
description: Explicit, owner-bound, expiring records that may suppress a SUPPRESSIBLE gate when status is approved and the record is unexpired. Consumed by scripts/enforce-gates.sh (v0.1.3+). v0.1.8+: records are FINDING-SCOPED by default (match rule_id+files; implemented for unsafe_docker against reports/raw/hadolint.json); broad gate-wide suppression requires explicit "scope":"gate" and is discouraged. Raw counts are never reduced. Never suppresses secrets/expired_exceptions/missing_release_evidence.

gate.description: Gate this record targets. Only 'unsafe_docker' and 'medium_vulnerabilities' are honored as suppressions in v0.1.3; others are ignored.

scope.description: v0.1.8+. 'finding' (DEFAULT): suppress only findings matching rule_id/files (implemented for unsafe_docker). 'gate': BROAD — suppress the whole gate (discouraged; reported as broad). A record with neither scope nor rule_id/files is ambiguous and does NOT suppress.

rule_id.description: v0.1.8+. Single tool rule to match for finding scope. unsafe_docker sources (v0.1.10): Hadolint rule ids (DL3018/DL3008/DL3016/DL4006/…) from reports/raw/hadolint.json, and 'SS_DOCKER_BASE_DIGEST' (the Docker base-image digest detector) from reports/raw/docker-base-digest.json. A DL3018 record does NOT match SS_DOCKER_BASE_DIGEST findings (and vice versa). Omit to match any rule within the listed files.

rule_ids.description: v0.1.8+. Optional list form of rule_id (a finding matches if its code is in this list).

files.description: v0.1.8+. File paths the finding-scope record applies to (e.g. 'Dockerfile', 'Dockerfile.prod', 'docker/8.3/Dockerfile'). Matched by exact path, path suffix, or basename. Omit to match any file for the given rule.

components.description: v0.1.8 RESERVED (not yet enforced). Component identifiers for non-Docker finding scoping.

fingerprints.description: v0.1.8 RESERVED (not yet enforced). Stable per-finding fingerprints for exact matching.

expires_at.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD). A record only suppresses while expires_at >= today.

scanner.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Scanner whose finding this record accepts (e.g. 'osv-scanner', 'grype', 'trivy'). scripts/enforce-security-policy.sh matches a finding only when its scanner equals this value — a record for the wrong scanner does NOT suppress the finding.

category.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Finding category the record accepts (e.g. 'dependency_vulnerabilities'). Must equal the finding's category to match.

finding_id.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Narrow finding identifier this record accepts. Required for a finding-scoped production waiver (no broad scanner/package suppression).

issue.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). REQUIRED issue/tracking reference (URL or id) linking the accepted risk to a remediation ticket. A record without it is rejected by the production gate.

created_at.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Record creation date. (expires_at - created_at) must not exceed the policy max waiver lifetime.

emergency.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). Marks this as an emergency-release acceptance: short-lived, requires an approver and an incident reference, and may accept a critical finding the normal path would block.

incident.description: v1.11+ (production-security-policy). REQUIRED for emergency records: the incident reference the emergency release is tracked under.
